title: Slat/Cage Armor Calibration — HEAT Warhead Disruption

test_cases.notes: Slat armor vs RPG-7 PG-7V at tactical standoff ~300mm. Slat armor bars crush the ogive, damage the shaped charge liner, or short the piezoelectric fuze, preventing proper jet formation. Expected disruption probability ~80-90% with adequate bar spacing (gap < warhead diameter). Equivalent area density ~20% of RHA for KE threats, but disruption mechanism is primary.
Reduced standoff (150mm) still disrupts — bar directly impacts the warhead nose cone. Slat armor RHAe of 0.20 reflects minimal KE resistance but disruption mechanism is the primary defeat mode.
KE performance limit of slat armor: thin steel bars offer ~20% RHAe, so 6mm slat bar ≈ 1.2mm RHAe vs KE. Light ball rounds may perforate individual bars. The primary role remains HEAT disruption.

Cage armor vs RPG-7: lightweight wire mesh defeats HEAT by crushing the ogive or shorting the fuze. Lower RHAe (0.15) than slat armor due to thinner wire. Disruption probability ~60-75% depending on mesh spacing and wire gauge. Common on light tactical vehicles.

references: US ARL-TR-1233 'Evaluation of Standoff Armor for Light Vehicles'
Jane's Armour and Artillery Upgrades — Cage/Slat Armor Systems
